generated_pair.question: What is the name of the sample database provided by Snowflake?
supporting_sentences: The sample database, SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA, is identical to the databases that you create in your account, except that it is read-only.
CREATE DATABASE SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA FROM SHARE SFC_SAMPLES.SAMPLE_DATA;

generated_pair.question: What operations are not allowed on the SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA database?
generated_pair.answer: DDL operations on the data set schemas, DML operations on the tables, and cloning or Time Travel on the database or any schemas/tables in the database.
supporting_sentences: No DDL can be performed on the data set schemas (i.e. tables and other database objects cannot be added, dropped, or altered).
No DML can be performed on the tables in the schemas.
No cloning or Time Travel can be performed on the database or any schemas/tables in the database.

generated_pair.question: How can you create the SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA database if it is not available in your account?
generated_pair.answer: Execute the SQL statements to create the database from the share and grant the PUBLIC role access to it.
supporting_sentences: If the database has not been created for your account and you want access to it, execute the following SQL statements with the ACCOUNTADMIN role active:
-- Create a database from the share. CREATE DATABASE SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA FROM SHARE SFC_SAMPLES.SAMPLE_DATA;
-- Grant the PUBLIC role access to the database. GRANT IMPORTED PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA TO ROLE PUBLIC;

generated_pair.question: What is the fully-qualified name of the shared database as shown in the 'origin' column of the SHOW DATABASES output?
supporting_sentences: The origin column in the SHOW DATABASES output (or the Origin column in the Databases page in the interface) displays the fully-qualified name of the shared database, SFC_SAMPLES.SAMPLE_DATA, indicating it originated from the SFC_SAMPLES account (used by Snowflake to share the sample data).

generated_pair.question: What are the two methods to query a table or view in the SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA database?
generated_pair.answer: Reference the fully-qualified name of the table in your query or specify the sample database and schema for your session using the USE DATABASE and/or USE SCHEMA commands.
supporting_sentences: To use a table or view in the sample database, you can either: Reference the fully-qualified name of the table in your query (in the form of snowflake_sample_data.schema_name.object_name). OR Specify the sample database (and schema) for your session using the USE DATABASE and/or USE SCHEMA commands.
